findings.description: Page footers and decorative elements are injected inline into the main content, breaking the reading order and splitting paragraphs/list items.
findings.evidence: On HTML page 3, the text of the 'Environmental' bullet point is interrupted by the page footer text ('State of North Carolina...') and a decorative gradient bar before continuing with the rest of the sentence. This issue occurs across multiple pages.

findings.description: Tabular layout of memo metadata is lost.
findings.evidence: On PDF page 1, the Date, To, From, and Subject fields are aligned in a structured format. In the HTML, this alignment is completely lost, and the text is rendered as a single vertical column of blocks.

findings.description: Header content is duplicated and page elements are placed out of order.
findings.evidence: On HTML page 1, the right-aligned text block starting with 'PAT McCRORY' appears correctly at the top right, but then appears a second time further down the page, immediately following an improperly injected footer and gradient bar.
